statement.en: CRYPTOCURRENCY REGULATION: A DESCRIPTIVE CASE STUDY ON THE POLICIES OF INDIA, VIETNAM, AND THE REPUBLIC OF THE PHILIPPINES
statement.summary: AbstractThe impact of cryptocurrency on the global economy is enormous. It has brought about changes both positive and negative. However, the advantages of cryptocurrency are far-reaching for many of its users who experience financial and economic inequality, causing its prevalence in lower-middle income countries. This development has caused the governments in these countries to tackle the complicated legal implications that follow. This paper aims to examine the regulatory trajectories and compile the legislation put in place in the countries of India, Vietnam and the Republic of Philippines and how each country has reckoned with this asset. Keywords: cryptocurrency, digital currency, digital assets, policy, instruments, regulation, legal treatment

proposer: Dr.M.Ramya Sree, Kamakshi Kashvi Kotagiri, Sai Priya Lalana Agastyaraju et al.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: This is a descriptive case study (2026-04-25, future-dated) analyzing existing regulatory frameworks across three jurisdictions. The axiom is provisional because: (1) cryptocurrency regulation is actively evolving in all three countries cited, (2) the paper is dated post-publication review, (3) regulatory status oscillates between bans, restrictions, and legalization attempts, (4) no single stable truth-value applies across the three cases simultaneously—each jurisdiction holds different regulatory positions

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 不平等が積分される速度が、規制の遅れを超えるとき、包摂性が現れる。
